question:
Will CA Vélez Sarsfield win on 2025-11-01?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for November 1, 2025
If CA Vélez Sarsfield wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.afa.com.ar/

evidences.title: Club Atlético Vélez Sarsfield - Wikipedia
evidences.content: On its way to the championship, the team had finished first in their group, ahead Boca Juniors (with results of 1–1 and 2–1), and Brazilians Palmeiras (1–0 and 1–4) and Cruzeiro (1–1 and 2–0). Subsequently, Vélez had defeated Uruguayan Defensor Sporting in the round of 16 (1–1 and 0–0, 4–3 in penalties), Venezuelan Minervén in the quarter-finals (0–0 and 2–0) and Colombian Junior in the semi-finals (1–2 and 2–1, 5–4 in penalties). [...] Moreover, Omar Wehbe was league top scorer with 16 goals. In total, the team played 17 games, winning 11, drawing 3, and losing another 3; with 44 goals for and 17 against. evidences.title: Stoppage Time Rules in Soccer
evidences.content: # Stoppage Time Rules in Soccer ## Understand Soccer 9430 subscribers 15 likes ### Description 1266 views Posted: 28 Jun 2025 Video on Penalty Kick Rules: Video on Throw In Rules: Link to Soccer Coaching Book on Amazon: Let’s cover STOPPAGE TIME RULES IN SOCCER. Stoppage time, often referred to as injury time or ADDED TIME, is additional playing time added to each half of a soccer match. Contrary to popular belief, it's not just added for injuries. It serves various purposes. According to Law 7 of the FIFA Laws of the Game, the referee has the authority to make allowances for time lost during the half due to: Substitutions, injuries, time-wasting, and any other significant stoppages. [...] The referee determines the amount of stoppage time, and it's typically displayed on a board on the sideline. This board indicates the minimum amount of ADDED TIME, and the referee can decide to extend it further, if necessary. Additional time is often added if there is an injury in stoppage time. Stoppage time is often a source of tension, especially when a team is trailing or when there's a contentious incident late in the game. However, the decision lies solely with the referee, who must con...

evidences.title: What is stoppage time? Know the rule for 2026 World Cup - Yahoo Sports
evidences.content: ## 2026 World Cup: 20 players who will get you out of your seat ## 2026 World Cup: 20 players who will get you out of your seat ## 2026 World Cup: 20 players who will get you out of your seat ## What is stoppage time? Stoppage time is the extra minutes added to the end of each half in a soccer match. The referees keep tabs on how much time was spent during regulation not being played, and then add that amount of time to the end of the half. Stoppage time is often written with a plus sign and however many additional minutes. So for example, if two minutes of stoppage time is added, it's written "90+2," but the game clock will just keep counting up until "92:00."

evidences.title: Professional Soccer Timing: Stoppage, Extra Time, Shootouts
evidences.content: behind the final whistle, it's important to see why every minute matters. Standard Match Length and Structure A standard professional soccer match consists of 90 minutes, divided into two halves of 45 minutes each, with a 15-minute halftime break. This structure is commonly observed in various leagues and tournaments. In knockout-style tournaments, if teams are tied after the regulation time, they proceed to extra time, which comprises two additional halves of 15 minutes each. Should the score remain level after extra time, a penalty shootout ensues. During the shootout, each team alternates five penalty kicks to determine the winner. This format is designed to consistently produce a result, thus ensuring that a victor is established in each match, which is significant in competitive [...] influence on match dynamics, compelling teams and coaches to continuously adjust their strategies, particularly during stoppage time, which can be unpredictable. Managers often modify their tactical approach—either defensively or offensively—based on the urgency created by these additional minutes of play. In tournament-style matches, the introduction of extra time necessitates careful managem...

Foul: A violation of a rule resulting in a stoppage of the game clock and the awarding of a free throw. There are two types of fouls: Physical fouls (physical contact of a player preventing an opposing player from continuing with movement) Technical fouls (against rules, e.g., false start or restart, to strike the ball with clenched fist, two hands, etc.) Ball under: Ordinary foul called against a player for taking the ball under water when tackled by an opponent or with intent to hide the ball [...] or shot clock and continues after every stoppage when the player puts the ball into play according to the Rules, shoots or passes the ball. evidences.title: What is added time in football?
evidences.content: ## What is added time in football? Added time, also called injury time or stoppage time, is implemented by the referee at the end of each half to make up for time lost due to substitutions, disciplinary penalties (yellow and red cards), injury to players, arguments with the referee and delays due to video assistant referee (VAR) check. Excessive celebration after a goal or time-wasting tactics used by teams are also considered by the referee before stoppage time is added. The referee or one of his assistants keeps a track of the time that is lost when play is stopped due to these reasons and adds it at the end of regulation time - after 45 minutes and 90 minutes. [...] # What is 'added time' in football: Know how it is calculated Added time is also referred to as injury time or stoppage time. It is implemented by the match officials to make up for lost time during a football match. GettyImages-1431695195 Picture by 2022 Getty Images By Aarish Ansari 24 November 2022 07:16 GMT-54 min read A typical football game lasts for 90 minutes with two halves of 45 minutes. However, the clock often goes past the designated time at the end of each half, with an addition of a few minutes. The...

evidences.title: Argentina wastes a window while preparing to defend its World Cup title - The Athletic
evidences.content: Yet when The Athletic asked an AFA representative whether VMG would continue to be AFA’s U.S.-based promoter, the response was telling: “No, no.” AFA has been on a determined mission to expand its brand throughout North America and globally since 2017. The project made inroads in the U.S. after Argentina’s 2022 World Cup final win over France and continued with Messi’s MLS arrival the following year. Since then, AFA has begun construction on a training facility in the Miami area. During the 2024 Copa América in the U.S., Argentina played to sold-out crowds in Atlanta, New Jersey, Houston, and Miami, which established the national team brand as one of the most popular in the world. Argentina's Lautaro Martinez and Emiliano Martinez [...] This window proved that even Argentina and Messi are not guaranteed to sell out a U.S. stadium on a whim. But the mistakes were clear from the start.
